Module com.trifasico.x50zo
Package com.trifasico.x50zo.model.players

Class HumanPlayer

java.lang.Object
com.trifasico.x50zo.model.players.PlayerAdapter
com.trifasico.x50zo.model.players.HumanPlayer
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPlayer
public class HumanPlayer extends PlayerAdapter
Represents the human participant in a Cincuentazo game.

Extends PlayerAdapter and relies entirely on its hand-management implementation. The only responsibility this class adds is the selection strategy: the human picks a card by index, supplied externally by the UI layer (the controller calls playSelectedCard(int, int) after the player taps/clicks a card).

Design notes:

  • No AI logic lives here — card choice is always driven by the view.
  • The class is intentionally thin; any state that belongs to all players (hand, name, playability checks) is in PlayerAdapter.
  • The controller should call PlayerAdapter.hasPlayableCard(int) before enabling the "play" button so the user is never offered an illegal move.
Version:
1.0
Author:
Yostin Ramirez, Lesly Zapata, Joseph Terreros

  • Constructor Details

    • HumanPlayer

      public HumanPlayer(String name)
      Constructs a HumanPlayer with the given display name.
      Parameters:
      name - the human player's display name; must not be null or blank
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is blank

  • Method Details

    • playSelectedCard

      public Card playSelectedCard(int cardIndex, int currentSum) throws InvalidPlayException
      Plays the card at cardIndex from the human's hand.

      This is the primary entry point called by the controller when the human selects a card in the UI. Delegates validation and removal to PlayerAdapter.playCard(int, int), which throws InvalidPlayException if the card would push the table sum above 50.

      Typical controller usage:

      
 try {
     Card played = humanPlayer.playSelectedCard(selectedIndex, tableSum);
     tableSum += played.getValue(tableSum);
 } catch (InvalidPlayException e) {
     // show error feedback in the UI
 }
      Parameters:
      cardIndex - zero-based index of the card the human chose
      currentSum - the running table sum before this play
      Returns:
      the Card that was played and removed from the hand
      Throws:
      InvalidPlayException - if the selected card would exceed the sum of 50
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if cardIndex is outside [0, handSize)

    • getPlayableIndices

      public List<Integer> getPlayableIndices(int currentSum)
      Returns the indices of all cards in the human's hand that are legally playable given currentSum.

      The controller can use this list to highlight (or enable) only the cards the player may actually pick, providing visual feedback before a selection is made.

      Parameters:
      currentSum - the running table sum
      Returns:
      a list of zero-based indices of playable cards; empty if none
